title: edithea rupicola
intro: Edithea rupicola, also known as American pennywort, is a perennial aquatic plant native to North America, characterized by its oval-shaped leaves and yellow flowers. Traditionally, Edithea rupicola has been used in herbal medicine due to its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antispasmodic, and diuretic effects. The plant is believed to offer health benefits such as reducing blood pressure, alleviating symptoms of menopausal hot flashes, and supporting liver function. Edithea rupicola can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, or tinctures, allowing for various forms of consumption to harness its medicinal and health benefits.
